title: Agriculture, Forestry and Fishing
description: This section covers the utilization of plant and animal natural resources through farming, animal husbandry, and harvesting from natural environments.
inclusion: It includes activities such as crop cultivation, animal raising and breeding, and the collection of timber, wild plants, and animal products from farms or natural habitats.

title: Mining and Quarrying
description: This section includes the extraction of naturally occurring minerals in solid, liquid, or gaseous forms, using various methods such as underground mining, surface mining, and well operations, along with related preparation activities.
inclusion: It includes activities such as crushing, grinding, drying, sorting, liquefaction of natural gas, and agglomeration of solid fuels, typically carried out by the extracting units or nearby facilities.
exclusion: It excludes the processing of extracted materials (see Section C), use in construction without further transformation (see Section F), bottling of mineral waters (see 11.07), and treatment of certain minerals not done alongside extraction (see 23.9).

title: Manufacturing
description: This section includes the physical or chemical transformation of raw materials or components into new products, typically resulting in outputs ready for use or as inputs to further manufacturing.
inclusion: It includes activities such as substantial renovation, assembly of manufactured products, and the creation of new products from waste materials, such as silver from film waste or tyre retreading.
exclusion: It excludes waste recovery for secondary raw materials (see Section E), logging and beneficiating of agricultural products (see Section A), ore processing (see Section B), construction activities (see Section F), and transformation activities not resulting in new products (see Section G).

title: Electricity, Gas, Steam and Air Conditioning Supply
description: This section covers the provision and distribution of electricity, natural gas, steam, hot water, and air conditioning through a permanent infrastructure of networks such as lines, mains, and pipes.
inclusion: It includes the generation, control, and distribution of electric power, gas, steam, and air conditioning, including within industrial parks and residential buildings.
exclusion: It excludes the operation of water and sewerage utilities (see 36, 37) and the long-distance transport of gas through pipelines.

title: Water Supply; Sewerage, Waste Management and Remediation Activities
description: This section includes the collection, treatment, and disposal of waste and sewage, as well as the management of contaminated sites and the supply of water for various uses.
inclusion: It includes waste management for industrial and household waste, sewage treatment, and water supply activities often conducted alongside sewage treatment.

title: Construction
description: This section covers general and specialised construction activities for buildings and civil engineering works, including new projects, repairs, additions, and temporary structures, whether performed directly or through subcontracting.
inclusion: It includes the complete construction of buildings and civil engineering works, specialised construction activities as part of projects, repair of buildings, and development of construction projects for sale.

title: Wholesale and Retail Trade; Repair of Motor Vehicles and Motorcycles
description: This section includes the wholesale and retail sale of goods without transformation and related services, as well as the repair of motor vehicles and motorcycles.
inclusion: It includes activities like sorting, grading, bottling, packaging, and the resale of goods to businesses or the general public, along with repair services for motor vehicles.

title: Transportation and Storage
description: This section includes the transport of passengers or freight by various modes, along with related services such as cargo handling, storage, and postal and courier activities.
inclusion: It includes renting transport equipment with driver or operator and supporting transport activities like terminal operations and parking.
exclusion: It excludes major repair of transport equipment (see 33.1), construction and repair of transport infrastructure (see 42), motor vehicle maintenance (see 45.20), and transport equipment rental without driver (see 77.1, 77.3).

title: Accommodation and Food Service Activities
description: This section covers short-term accommodation services for travelers and the preparation and serving of meals and drinks for immediate consumption.
exclusion: It excludes long-term residential accommodation (see Section L) and the preparation of food or drinks not intended for immediate consumption or sold through wholesale/retail channels (see Section C).

title: Information and Communication
description: This section includes the creation, publishing, and distribution of information and cultural content, telecommunications, IT services, and data processing activities.

title: Financial and Insurance Activities
description: This section includes activities related to financial services, insurance and pension funding, and asset-holding entities such as holding companies and trusts.

title: Real Estate Activities
description: This section includes activities related to real estate sales, rentals, management, and related services, carried out either on owned or leased property or on a contract basis.

title: Professional, Scientific and Technical Activities
description: This section includes specialised services requiring high levels of expertise, such as legal, accounting, engineering, and scientific research services.

title: Administrative and Support Service Activities
description: This section includes support services for general business operations that do not primarily involve the transfer of specialised knowledge, such as employment services, security, and facility management.

title: Public Administration and Defence; Compulsory Social Security
description: This section includes government-related activities such as legislation, taxation, national defence, public order, immigration, foreign affairs, and compulsory social security administration.
exclusion: It excludes activities like teaching or healthcare, even if conducted by public entities (see Sections P and Q).

title: Education
description: This section includes all levels and types of education, from preschool to higher education, including adult and special education, whether provided publicly or privately, through various formats such as in-person or online.

title: Human Health and Social Work Activities
description: This section includes medical care by health professionals, residential care involving health support, and social work activities without health care involvement.

title: Arts, Entertainment and Recreation
description: This section includes cultural, artistic, entertainment, and recreational activities for the general public, including live shows, museums, gambling, sports, and leisure facilities.

title: Other Service Activities
description: This section includes a variety of personal services not classified elsewhere, such as those provided by membership organisations and the repair of computers and household goods.

title: Activities of Households as Employers; Undifferentiated Goods- and Services-producing Activities of Households for Own Use
description: This section includes households’ subsistence production of goods and services for their own use, when no primary activity can be identified and the output is not for market sale.

title: Activities of Extraterritorial Organisations and Bodies
description: This section includes the activities of international organisations such as the UN, IMF, World Bank, and diplomatic missions determined by the host country location.
